Title: Promiscuous terpene synthases from Prunella vulgaris highlight the importance of substrate and compartment switching in terpene synthase evolution

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranylgeranyl diphosphate (GGPP) to 11-hydroxy vulgarisane, the likely biosynthetic precursor of vulgarisins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms likely biosynthetic precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; identified as the first step consistent with the proposed vulgarisin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of 11-hydroxy vulgarisane and thereby supports vulgarisin biosynthesis in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and Escherichia coli; in vitro enzyme assay with GGPP; GC-MS; NMR structural elucidation; UHPLC/MS metabolite detection; GFP localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Among the four most highly expressed root TPS transcripts; root-enriched based on transcriptome selection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root transcript detected; vulgarisin-related metabolites detected in roots but not leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; in vitro assays with multiple prenyl diphosphate substrates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvHVS is a plastidial TPS-a enzyme from Prunella vulgaris. 2. PvHVS uses GGPP to produce 11-hydroxy vulgarisane, confirmed by NMR. 3. 11-hydroxy vulgarisane is the likely precursor and first identified step toward vulgarisin diterpenoids. 4. PvHVS also shows promiscuous activity with other prenyl diphosphates, supporting substrate switching in TPS evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also active on (E,E)-FPP, producing putative bisabolol/farnesene, and shows minor unidentified GGPP-derived diterpene product; first reported diterpene synthase in TPS-a subfamily from Lamiaceae
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for metabolic engineering of rare vulgarisane diterpenoids and for reconstructing the vulgarisin pathway; candidate gene for pathway stacking with downstream P450 and acyltransferases
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Root transcriptome SRA: SRR766790; Nanopore root transcriptome PRJNA491730; leaf transcriptome SRA: SRR5150718
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression vector in Nicotiana benthamiana

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: root sesquiterpenes (including products consistent with β-barbatene-type tricyclic sesquiterpenes)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes primarily (E,E)-farnesyl diphosphate into tricyclic sesquiterpenes, annotated including β-barbatene-type products; also acts on GPP, NPP and NNPP
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Tricyclic sesquiterpenes including a product annotated as β-barbatene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely functions as a major root sesquiterpene synthase in P. vulgaris
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; in vitro enzyme assays; heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and Escherichia coli; GC-MS; GFP localization; comparison with root metabolite profile
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Second most highly expressed TPS in root RNA-seq data
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in roots; product profile resembles root sesquiterpene metabolite pattern
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assayed with multiple prenyl diphosphate substrates in vitro and in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvTPS5 is a cytosolic TPS-a enzyme highly expressed in roots. 2. It uses (E,E)-FPP to produce mainly tricyclic sesquiterpenes. 3. Its recombinant product ratio resembles the sesquiterpene pattern of P. vulgaris roots, suggesting native root function. 4. It is promiscuous and can also use GPP, NPP and NNPP.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In N. benthamiana with plastid targeting and (E,E)-FPPS coexpression, produced an additional unknown product q absent in vitro; active on NNPP in both E. coli and N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering diverse sesquiterpene scaffolds and studying product-determining residues in TPS-a enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Root transcriptome SRA: SRR766790; Nanopore root transcriptome PRJNA491730; leaf transcriptome SRA: SRR5150718
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression vector; transit peptide added for plastid-targeted assays in Nicotiana benthamiana

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Uses multiple prenyl diphosphate substrates; from (E,E)-FPP produces acyclic sesquiterpene alkenes and alcohols, and from GGPP produces a minor unidentified diterpene
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: (E,E)-farnesyl diphosphate ((E,E)-FPP); also active on GGPP, (Z,Z)-FPP and NPP
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Acyclic sesquiterpene alkenes and alcohols from (E,E)-FPP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms terpene products, but native end product connection not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Encodes a promiscuous plastidial TPS-a enzyme, but its native metabolite role in P. vulgaris roots was not resolved
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; in vitro enzyme assays; heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and Escherichia coli; GC-MS; GFP localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most highly expressed TPS gene in root RNA-seq data
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in roots, but recombinant products could not be linked to detected root metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assayed with multiple prenyl diphosphate substrates in vitro and in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvTPS2 is a plastidial TPS-a enzyme among the most highly expressed root TPSs. 2. It is promiscuous, accepting multiple substrates of different chain lengths and stereochemistries. 3. It produces mainly acyclic sesquiterpenes from (E,E)-FPP and a minor unidentified diterpene from GGPP. 4. Its native metabolic function remains unresolved because its products were not connected to root metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors could not identify a preferred native substrate; products may be rapidly turned over or require an untested substrate
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool for generating unusual terpene products and for probing determinants of substrate promiscuity, but native pathway relevance is uncertain
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Root transcriptome SRA: SRR766790; Nanopore root transcriptome PRJNA491730; leaf transcriptome SRA: SRR5150718

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Uses several prenyl diphosphate substrates; from (E,E)-FPP forms a bicyclic sesquiterpene annotated as d-cadinene in vitro, and from GPP/NPP forms monoterpene mixtures including limonene and γ-terpinene
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: (E,E)-farnesyl diphosphate ((E,E)-FPP); also active on GPP, NPP and (Z,Z)-FPP
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: d-cadinene-like bicyclic sesquiterpene from (E,E)-FPP in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Encodes a cytosolic promiscuous TPS-a enzyme producing bicyclic sesquiterpenes and monoterpenes depending on substrate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; in vitro enzyme assays; heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and Escherichia coli; GC-MS; GFP localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Among four most highly expressed root TPS transcripts selected for study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected from root transcriptome candidate set
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assayed with multiple prenyl diphosphate substrates; transit peptide added for plastid-targeted N. benthamiana assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvTPS4 is a cytosolic TPS-a enzyme from P. vulgaris. 2. It is promiscuous, accepting (E,E)-FPP, GPP, NPP and (Z,Z)-FPP. 3. It forms a d-cadinene-like bicyclic sesquiterpene from (E,E)-FPP in vitro and monoterpene mixtures from GPP/NPP. 4. Its native end products in P. vulgaris were not definitively assigned.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Produced an additional unknown product q in N. benthamiana only when coexpressed with plastid-targeted (E,E)-FPPS; not detected in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme for combinatorial biosynthesis of diverse terpene skeletons and mechanistic studies of TPS product cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Root transcriptome SRA: SRR766790; Nanopore root transcriptome PRJNA491730; leaf transcriptome SRA: SRR5150718
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression vector; transit peptide added for plastid-targeted assays in Nicotiana benthamiana
